name: Chocolate Cake with Chocolate Almond Brittle
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Bittersweet or Semisweet Chocolate; chopped
12 ounces Bittersweet or Semisweet Chocolate; chopped
recipeInstructions:
For Brittle:
1. Line a 13x9x2-inch baking pan with foil.
2. Melt chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth. Pour chocolate into the prepared pan and spread evenly. Sprinkle almonds over the chocolate.
3. Refrigerate until the chocolate sets, about 30 minutes. Break brittle into irregular shapes.
For Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Butter a 9-inch-diameter springform pan with 2-3/4-inch-high sides and line the bottom with parchment paper. Wrap the outside of the pan with foil.
3. Melt chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth. Set aside to cool.
4. Using an electric mixer, beat butter and 1/3 cup sugar in a large bowl until blended. Mix in cornstarch, espresso powder, and salt. Beat in egg yolks one at a time. Mix in melted chocolate.
5. Using clean dry beaters, beat egg whites and 1/3 cup sugar in another large bowl until stiff but not dry. Fold the egg whites into the chocolate mixture in two additions.
6. Transfer to prepared pan and bake until set and a tester inserted into the center comes out with moist crumbs attached, about 50 minutes. Cool completely on a rack (the cake may fall and appear cracked).
7. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 4 hours.
For Serving:
Preheat oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit. Bake cake uncovered just until warm, about 20 minutes. Cut around the cake to loosen. Release pan sides. Cut warm cake into wedges. Garnish with pieces of brittle and serve.
